mnemonics: S.E.T. Your Forecasts Straight: Standard Errors Tell the probability Tale.
keyPoints: Forecasts are calculated using the estimated model (e.g., ARIMA) to project future values based on past data.
Probability limits (confidence intervals) are built around a forecast using the forecast value ± (multiple of the standard error of forecast).
The standard error of the forecast increases for periods further into the future, making the probability limits wider.
commonMistakes: Using the residuals' standard deviation instead of the forecast standard error to calculate probability limits.
Forgetting that the forecast error variance compounds over time, leading to underestimating the uncertainty of long-term forecasts.
Assuming the probability limits are symmetric and normal without checking the model's assumptions about the error distribution.
